original_hypothesis.title: Forced Hook Execution Causes O(N) Gas Griefing on Transfers
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When a market requires withdrawal access, the hook contract forcibly enables the `Bit_Enabled_Transfer` flag. This causes the market to call `onTransfer` for every token transfer. Even if `transferRequiresAccess` is false, the hook still calls `_tryValidateAccessInner` for any recipient who is not a known lender. If the recipient lacks a credential, this triggers `_loopTryGetCredential`, making an external `staticcall` to EVERY registered pull provider. An attacker can exploit this by transferring 1 wei to many uncredentialed addresses, or the sheer baseline gas cost of multiple external calls could break DEX composability.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Forced execution of credential validation loops on unrestricted transfers creates massive, unintended gas overhead.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _tryValidateAccessInner called even when transferRequiresAccess is false

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: O(N) external calls per transfer where N is number of pull providers
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Network congestion griefing or breaking competitor DEX pools

economic_questions: What is the gas cost per transfer with 5-10 pull providers?
Does this exceed standard DEX router gas limits?
validation_requirements: Measure gas consumption of onTransfer with multiple pull providers
Test integration with Uniswap V2/V3 routers

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Gas cost scaling with pull provider count

validation_result.mechanism: Forced O(N) external calls on unrestricted transfers
validation_result.impact_summary: Every transfer to a non-known-lender recipient triggers O(N) staticcalls to pull providers even when transferRequiresAccess is false, creating unnecessary gas overhead that can degrade token composability.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis correctly identifies a chain of code behaviors that combine to create excessive gas consumption:

1. When `useOnQueueWithdrawal` is true, `Bit_Enabled_Transfer` is forcibly enabled (FixedTermLoanHooks._onCreateMarket), causing `onTransfer` to be called for every token transfer.

2. `transferRequiresAccess` is set from the original `useOnTransfer` flag BEFORE the forced flag merge, so it can be `false` while transfers are still hooked.

3. In `onTransfer`, for any recipient who is not a known lender, `_tryValidateAccessInner` is unconditionally called regardless of `transferRequiresAccess`.

4. For an uncredentialed recipient with no hooksData, `_tryValidateAccessInner` falls through to `_loopTryGetCredential`, which makes a staticcall to every registered pull provider.

5. After all this work, if `transferRequiresAccess` is false, the transfer succeeds regardless of the credential check result.

The code explicitly comments that this is intentional ('the recipient may need to be updated to reflect their new status as a known lender'), but the loop over ALL pull providers is wasteful when no credential is found and the transfer succeeds anyway. This constitutes a code defect: unnecessary external calls that scale linearly with the number of pull providers, with no early-exit optimization when the result is irrelevant to the transfer's success.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof-of-signal at AccessControlHooks.sol is confirmed. The `onTransfer` function unconditionally calls `_tryValidateAccessInner` for non-known-lender recipients. The same pattern exists in FixedTermLoanHooks.sol. Both contracts exhibit identical behavior where the transfer hook is forcibly enabled by withdrawal access requirements, and `_tryValidateAccessInner` performs O(N) external calls even when the transfer doesn't require access control.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When useOnQueueWithdrawal is true but useOnTransfer is false, transferRequiresAccess is set to false but the Transfer hook is forcibly enabled. This means onTransfer is called for every transfer even though access isn't required.

_tryValidateAccessInner is unconditionally called regardless of transferRequiresAccess. For uncredentialed recipients, this triggers the full pull provider loop, consuming gas proportional to the number of pull providers.
This loop iterates over ALL pull providers, making a staticcall(gas(), providerAddress, ...) for each one. For uncredentialed addresses that will never have credentials, every call is wasted gas.

Each call passes gas() (all remaining gas) to the external provider via staticcall. A single external call costs at minimum ~2600 gas (cold STATICCALL), plus the provider's execution cost. With N providers, this is at least 2600*N additional gas per transfer.

calling_sequence: Market contract calls onTransfer on hook contract (triggered because Bit_Enabled_Transfer was forcibly set)
onTransfer checks !isKnownLenderOnMarket[to][msg.sender] - true for new recipients
_tryValidateAccessInner: status has no credential -> lastProvider is EmptyRoleProvider (isNull=true)
_loopTryGetCredential: for each pull provider, calls _tryGetCredential which makes staticcall to provider
All calls return no credential -> _loopTryGetCredential returns false
onTransfer: market.transferRequiresAccess is false, so no revert
Transfer succeeds after O(N) unnecessary external calls
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The complete path starts at the market calling onTransfer (because Bit_Enabled_Transfer was forcibly set during market creation). For any recipient not yet a known lender, _tryValidateAccessInner is called. When the recipient has no credential and no hooksData is provided, the function cascades through all validation steps and ultimately calls _loopTryGetCredential, which iterates over all pull providers making external staticcalls. Each staticcall costs at minimum ~2600 gas (cold account access). With 10 pull providers, this adds ~26,000+ gas per transfer to any new recipient, even when transferRequiresAccess is false and the validation result is irrelevant.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that are relevant to market configuration. The LibDeployment.sol is a generic deployment utility.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The scenario described is valid: a market with useOnQueueWithdrawal=true but useOnTransfer=false will have onTransfer forcibly enabled via Bit_Enabled_Transfer but transferRequiresAccess=false. Every transfer to a new recipient triggers O(N) external calls to pull providers. This increases gas cost per transfer proportional to the number of registered pull providers. While the attacker pays their own gas for dust transfers, the real impact is on all legitimate token holders who transfer tokens - they pay elevated gas costs. This particularly impacts DEX composability where gas limits on router calls may be exceeded. The borrower controls the number of pull providers via addRoleProvider, so the severity depends on deployment configuration, but the code defect exists regardless.
validation_result.impact_assessment: The impact is a gas efficiency defect that scales with the number of pull providers. For markets with withdrawal access requirements but no transfer access requirements, every transfer to a new address incurs unnecessary gas overhead. With 5-10 pull providers, this could add 13,000-26,000+ gas per transfer (cold staticcalls). While this may not break most transactions, it degrades gas efficiency and could cause issues with DEX routers that impose gas limits. The defect is that the code performs O(N) external calls when the result is irrelevant to the transaction's success.
recommended_mitigations: Skip _loopTryGetCredential in _tryValidateAccessInner when called from a context where access is not required (e.g., pass a flag indicating transferRequiresAccess)

In onTransfer, only call _tryValidateAccessInner when transferRequiresAccess is true; for non-access-required transfers, only check hooksData-provided credentials to opportunistically mark known lenders
Add a gas limit cap on individual provider staticcalls in _tryGetCredential instead of passing gas()
Consider limiting the maximum number of pull providers that can be registered
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy AccessControlHooks with a borrower. 2. Add 10 pull providers (mock contracts that return 0 for getCredential). 3. Create a market with useOnQueueWithdrawal=true and useOnTransfer=false. 4. Transfer market tokens to a new address with no credential. 5. Measure gas consumed by the transfer. 6. Compare with a market that has 0 pull providers. 7. Verify gas difference scales linearly with number of providers.
uncertainties: Exact gas cost per staticcall depends on provider contract code size and whether the account is warm/cold
Whether real-world deployments would have enough pull providers to cause meaningful gas overhead
Whether the design intention of opportunistically marking known lenders justifies the gas cost
Market contracts (not in scope) may have their own gas optimizations or limits that interact with this behavior
additional_testing_needed: Gas benchmarking of onTransfer with varying numbers of pull providers (1, 5, 10, 20)
Integration testing with common DEX routers to verify gas limit compatibility
Comparison of gas costs between markets with and without forced transfer hooks
Testing the scenario where transferRequiresAccess=false but onTransfer is forcibly enabled
